entry.title: What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Custom Widget: QRC3 2024 Release
entry.content:
Purpose

This blog introduces the latest updates of Custom Widgets features within SAP Analytics Cloud, as part of the QRC3 2024 release.

Overview

1. Custom Widget: Showing unbooked data values

Previously, the query result returned for the custom widget doesn't include unbooked data. However, displaying unbooked values is crucial. Hence, we're introducing this feature that incorporates unbooked data in the query results which can be utilized by custom widgets. this enhancement allows customers to effectively use unbooked data in their custom widgets.

2. Custom Widget: Support for calculated and account-based dimensions

In the optimized story experience, you can now seamlessly create and utilize calculated or account-based dimensions for custom widgets. This feature is essential for customers who wish to combine dimensions, group them, or create account-based ones for use in custom widgets.

3. Custom Widget: Increasing the number of the hosted custom widget in SAC to 50

Beforehand, SAC user was able to host until 25 Custom Widget per tenant, with the QRC3 release we increased the number to 50 in order to offer more flexibility for custom widget developer to extend their visualisation and easily hosted into SAC and into their Stories.

For further information, visit our SAP Analytics Cloud Community pages to find more product information, best practices, and more. 

Check out our SAP Road Map Explorer to see more upcoming features of SAP Analytics Cloud.

Thanks for reading!

entry.title: Where can start learning how to use the performance tools in SAP Analytics Cloud?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Managing Performance in SAP Analytics Cloud learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey will provide you with the knowledge and skills required to be able to successfully manage performance in SAP Analytics Cloud. You will gain insight into SAP Analytics Cloud performance tools and troubleshooting performance issues in SAP Analytics Cloud stories.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, you will be able to describe the different elements of SAP Analytics Cloud architecture and how they can impact on performance, and the tools used to analyze performance issues. You will be able to create stories using performance best practice, use the performance tools to analyze performance, as well as troubleshoot common performance issues related to SAP Analytics Cloud story design.

Prerequisites

  • Acquiring Basic Scripting Skills to Extend Stories in SAP Analytics Cloud

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Sneak Peek in to SAP Analytics Cloud release for Q4 2024
entry.content:
Hard to believe we are already entering Q4 2024 and with that we give you a glimpse of some of the new features in SAP Analytics Cloud. We have over 50 features for you this quarter but we will first take a sneak peek in to our Top 5 features in QRC4 2024. A full list of what is coming can be found on our Roadmap Explorer.

Bulk migration from classic stories to the optimized experience

The optimized story experience improves performance within SAP Analytics Cloud stories. We have enabled numerous features over the past couple of years and in this quarter we are excited to introduce support for the migration of multiple classic stories for SAP Analytics Cloud to the optimized experience. Some of the features include the ability to:

  • Overwrite the classic stories or save them as copies
  • Choose the location to save for converted stories
  • Get a log for the migration status

This new feature will help Customers save time and effort in migrating multiple stories and reduce migration downtime.

Grouping of analytics catalog assets

Another exciting new feature in our SAP Analytics Cloud catalog is the Quick filters in catalog to allow for faster and easier content discovery. End users can select a quick filter and search results get refreshed based on pre-defined filter sets. The quick filters are set by the admins for grouping content in a meaningful way and making the lives of end users easier. Feature is also available for catalog publishers and allows for consistent metadata tagging of content.

Plan Process Management - template support

An exciting new addition for our planning users is the introduction of a file repository template object for calendar processes. This enables planners to:

  • Save an existing process as a template (including subtasks and subprocesses)
  • Create a new process based on a template
  • Use templates in the event wizard to instantiate processes for multiple elements within the driving dimension
  • Support the transport of templates across tenants

 

 

Data Actions – dynamic planning area support

Staying with planning, in this release, we have taken further steps in our multi-phase refactoring of how user planning areas are created and managed. This is important as keeps the data size manageable for planning activities, which in turn improves performance and streamlines resource management.


This functionality has been building over the past few quarters in 2024, but specifically with the Q4 release, we are now supporting dynamic extension of existing planning area within a data action, while respecting boundaries defined when either DAC and/or data locking are enabled.


This new behavior applies to wherever the data action is triggered, and an existing planning area has been established, whether via stories, or the Excel Add-In

 

 

SAP Analytics Cloud System Overview Performance One Pager

Last but by no means least we bring you a Centralized Performance Dashboard. It provides a comprehensive, centralised solution for all relevant performance data, presented in a clear and organised visual format. This Performance One Pager consolidates key statistics from disparate tools into a unified view, eliminating the need for manual data gathering for administrators.

Learn more in this great blog by @thomas_fery 

 

There are plenty more exciting deliverables coming your way this quarter and we will be taking a deeper dive into these items in our What’s New video and blog series so stay tuned. For a full list of all features coming in QRC4 2024 for SAP Analytics Cloud check out the Roadmap Explorer

Want to experience new and existing features for yourself? Take the leap and start your journey towards making data-driven decisions with confidence by signing up for a free trial, today.

#sap #sapanalytics

entry.title: Understanding How to Retrieve IDs in SAC: A Step-by-Step Guide for Scripting and Debugging
entry.content:
I'm Filippo Naggi, an Enterprise Architect at Avvale with extensive experience across the entire planning landscape, from BPC to SAC, OneStream, and BigQuery.

 

In SAC, there are times when you need to retrieve an ID to use in filters. In this blog, we’ll explore how to do that. Follow along, as this will also help you understand how to debug effectively.

We'll set up a filter in a table and learn how to retrieve its technical name by using the debugging functionalities.

Let't build a table and setup a filter for 2022.

This is the table:

and this is the filter, 2022:

Now we could create a script which is getting executed each time the table will be selected.

 

Using the pop-up menu, let's ad a onSelect handler for the table:

 

Now we can write the script to get and log the filters.

Console.Log is used to log anything on your browser, for example on Chrome Developer Tool.

getDimensionFilters enable to extract a filter attached to a dimension.

 

This is the code:

Next step is saving our Story and switch to view mode.

Once in view mode, using F12, we can open Chrome Developer Tools.

After selecting the table, the handler will be executed.

Finallly we can look into the log, and extract the payload:

{
"value": "[Date].[YHQM].[Date.YEAR].[2022]",
"type": "Single"
}

Analyzing the payload, we notice the convention used for Date:

[Date].[YHQM].[Date.YEAR].[2022]

 

 

Thank you all for taking the time to read through this guide, I hope it helped clarify how to retrieve IDs and work with filters in SAC.

A special thanks to Boysie for the inspiration and brilliant idea that sparked this blog—your input made this deep dive possible.

 

Stay tuned for more tips and insights, and feel free to reach out if you have any questions or suggestions. Until next time!

entry.title: Mastering Variable-Driven Currency Conversion in SAP Analytics Cloud Planning
entry.content:
Hello,
I’m Filippo Naggi, an Enterprise Architect with a deep passion for enterprise technologies, specifically SAP SAC, SAP BPC, Onestream, and BigQuery. Today, I am excited to take you through a detailed guide on how to build a variable-driven measure for currency conversion within SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). This technique will enable you to select the desired destination currency dynamically and apply it seamlessly across various reports and analyses within your model.

Currency conversion is a critical aspect of financial modeling, especially when dealing with multi-currency datasets. Having the flexibility to switch between different currencies while maintaining consistency across measures is vital for making informed business decisions. In this blog, we will cover step-by-step how to create such a solution using a variable-driven approach in SAC.

Step 1: Model Setup

First, we’ll begin by setting up the model. Our model includes two dimensions, and we start with a source measure in USD (U.S. dollars), which will be our default currency for this exercise. As shown in Fig 1.x, this setup is foundational to ensure that our measures are properly aligned with the currency variable we will be adding later.

Fig. 1.1 The Multidimensional Model

Fig. 1.2 The Source measure in USD - Measure name: Translated

 

Step 2: Activating Currency Conversion

Next, we activate the built-in feature for currency conversion within SAC. SAC provides a robust framework to handle currency conversions dynamically, and by activating this feature, we can streamline the process. In Fig 2, you’ll see how the currency conversion option is enabled within the model, making it ready to support multiple currencies.

Fig.2 Currency Conversion Model Setup

 

 

Step 3: Building a Variable for Currency Conversion

The core of our approach lies in creating a variable that drives the currency conversion. This variable allows users to select the desired target currency, which can be applied universally throughout the system. In Fig 3, I will walk you through how to build and configure this variable step-by-step.

Fig.3 Currency Variable Settings

Step 4: Currency Rate Table

Of course, the actual conversion rates must be managed, and SAC simplifies this process through the use of a currency rate table. This table stores all the exchange rates that will be used in the conversion calculations. Fig 4 shows an example of how this table is configured and how the system pulls the relevant rates for each currency conversion.

Fig 4. Rates setup

 

 

Step 5: Applying the Variable in a Story

Once the variable is ready, it’s time to integrate it into a story. In this example, we’ll build a table in the story that leverages the currency conversion variable. SAC will automatically prompt users to select the currency at runtime. As shown in Fig 5, the system can even provide a default currency, which can be changed based on the user’s preferences.

Fig.5 Variable Story Input

 

 

 

 

Step 6: Runtime Selection of Different Currencies

The beauty of this solution is the flexibility it offers at runtime. Users can easily select different currencies based on the available options in the variable. As demonstrated in Fig 6.x, users can toggle between different currency selections, and the entire dataset will automatically adjust to reflect the newly selected currency.

Fig 6.1 USD Toggle

Fig 6.2 EUR Toggle

 

 

 

Step 7: Using the Feature in the Excel Plug-In

Lastly, it’s worth noting that this functionality isn’t limited to just the web interface of SAC. The variable-driven currency conversion can also be utilized through the SAC Excel plug-in. This allows users to work with currency conversions directly within their Excel environment, providing additional flexibility for analysis and reporting. Fig 7.x shows an example of how the feature operates within the plug-in.

Fig 7.1 Variable Selector - USD

Fig 7.2 Rate: USD

Fig 7.3 Variable Selector - EUR

Fig 7.4 Rate: EUR

 

 

Conclusion

In conclusion, variable-driven currency measures are a powerful feature within SAP Analytics Cloud, providing unmatched flexibility and control over financial reporting and analysis in multi-currency environments. This functionality highlights the multidimensional capabilities of SAC, making it an ideal tool for enterprise-scale financial planning and analysis.

If you have any questions or require further assistance in implementing this feature, feel free to reach out to me directly. I’d be happy to assist you in exploring the full potential of variable-driven currency conversion in your SAC environment.

Thank you for following along and Stefano for kicking off the interest in deep diving into this.

I hope you all found this guide helpful!

Best regards,
Filippo Naggi

entry.title: Manage Performance in SAP Analytic Cloud Stories - New Learning Journey
entry.content:
You are creating stories in SAP Analytics Cloud and want to make sure that they run with good performance?
Then check out our new Learning Journey "Managing Performance in SAP Analytics Cloud".

We will start with the basics on trouble shooting and introduce you the tools you can use in SAP Analytics Cloud. Then we will dive deeper into the following topics:

  • Optimizing Performance in Story Design
    The general design of a story has a huge impact on the performance, in this unit you see an example of a bad and good design taken from a real world customer example.
  • Optimizing Performance in Data Loading
    Have you ever wondered what the different "loading optimization settings" are and when to use which one? Are you sure you effectively use the "pause refresh" setting on widget level? If not this unit is made for you.
  • Optimizing Script Performance
    Creating your first script isn't a big deal but form a performance perspective a lot can go wrong. Get introduced to the Main- and Sandboxworker thread and that teaches you to write effective code.

The content will be updated with every new quarterly release.

After working through the learning journey and passing the embedded quizzes you can claim your "Record of Achievement".

SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: SAC for Defense Self Service Analytics
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) is a powerful and versatile analytical platform that can be leveraged for various use-cases in different organizational settings. Within the modern defense environment, in which data-driven decision making is more important than ever, SAC can help defense organizations handle the increasing volume of data generated from various sources, ranging from intelligence information to logistics. By leveraging SAC, defense organizations can improve their overall situational awareness, analyze material readiness and support capability-based planning, leveraging the full potential of the platform.

Situational Awareness

As a crucial cornerstone of operational oversight and planning, situational awareness allows defense leaders to understand and interpret the environment a given organization operates in. Enabled by the geospatial capabilities of the analytical platform, the user of SAC has the possibility to gain an overview of facilities, garrisons, infrastructure and any other geospatial data or points of interest they might deem important for their internal use. This data can be displayed and consumed on its own, or, as is the case in the image above, combined with other data sources for a more complete operational picture. 

Combined with data on readiness and capabilities, also relating to other use-case examples we will touch on below, the inclusion of spatial information (together with the possibility to add open data from various sources to the mix) provides a powerful and effective way to demonstrate real-time developments, together with the possibilities to focus, filter and combine data in an interactive manner through SAC's "Linked Analysis" feature.

Material Readiness

On the more granular level, when dealing with a given technical object or a group of technical objects, SAC provides the possibility to aggregate various indicators relating to these objects in its local calculations to be translated into KPIs and provide an operational overview when it comes to, e.g., material readiness, as seen on the picture above. In this specific case, we are dealing with a fleet of fighter jets grouped by tranche and weapon system they are equipped with, whose overall material readiness is calculated based on their total operational hours as well as their individual hours since last maintenance. This calculation can, however, be made to include more variables, as well as updating data based on live connections to an S/4HANA system which can be combined to calculate these key indicators directly in SAC.

Analytics Cloud allows the user to choose which underlying data they deem as important and then, subsequently, provides them with a possibility to combine them with different weights and parameters in the story itself to develop calculations that are then mirrored in graphs and charts to provide not only the user, but also a possibly wider audience with quite a complete and customized picture of what the overall readiness picture for, say, a group of fighters currently is, possibly also grouped by their tranche (see radar chart above) or weapon system (see heatmap above).

Mission Planning

Speaking of weights and parameters, this feature can also be carried over from the more granular analytic levels to high-level mission planning, such as in this third example of SAC for defense analytics. This dashboard focuses on the so-called Army Tactical Tasks (ARTs), which are all attributed with a preparation time and task seriousness, two indicators which are later combined with user-customizable weights and, based on these weights, grouped into categories of success/uncertainty/failure in real time.

This SAC story is a powerful example of the possibilities of data aggregation from the lowest organizational levels (the calculation of, e.g., preparation time, can start at the Force Element level already) to the highest planning levels that have the possibility to influence decision makers, but also provide those decision makers to have a say in how this aggregation impacts the data analysis, as we have seen with the example of weighted variables above.

Third party integration

One a more technical level, SAC also allows for various integrations of custom applications, such as webapps (as seen on the right side of the image above in a HANA-based chat interface, which is a part of a larger use-case related to disaster management: Smart Drone Logistics - SAP Media Share), as well as R visualizations and pop-ups that can be added to dashboards to enhance the analytical products of the given user.

Another important technical feature of SAP Analytics Cloud is the customizable scripting feature which utilizes built-in JavaScript capabilities to allow analytics users to create more complex versions of their stories, enable Role-Based Access Control and more complex data filtering. For example, below is a piece of JavaScript code that ensures switching between a GeoMap and a Table by clicking a radio button toggle on a given page of an SAC story:

 

var selectedValue = RadioButtonGroup_1.getSelectedKey();  // Get the selected value from the Radio Button widget

// Check the selected value to determine which widget to show
if (selectedValue === "Table") {
	// Show Table and Hide Map
	Table_1.setVisible(true);
	GeoMap_1.setVisible(false);
	console.log("Showing Table_1 and hiding Map_1.");
} else if (selectedValue === "Map") {
	// Show Map and Hide Table
	Table_1.setVisible(false);
	GeoMap_1.setVisible(true);
	console.log("Showing Map_1 and hiding Table_1.");
} else {
	// Handle unexpected values (optional)
	console.log(selectedValue);
}

 

Scripting allows users to create stories that are dynamic, well-optimized and up-to-date, while maintaining an already high level of analytical capabilities that are available out of the box.

Additional details

What we have not touched on in this specific blog is the different connection options that SAC utilizes in order to connect to an S/4 system, a Datasphere tenant or other possible data sources, as well as the different possible formats in which this data can be carried over into the platform (e.g., live connections or replication flows), as well as the details of how 3rd party data can be added to SAC (be it through simple Excel files, points of interest or the Data Marketplace feature on Datasphere). More information on these technical details of SAC can be expected in subsequent future blogs to come.

entry.title: Automating SAP DSP Task Chains with CLI Wrapping: FastAPI Integration with SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Introduction

When working with SAP Datasphere, triggering task chains is often a crucial part of automating data processes. SAP Datasphere provides the ability to start task chains using CLI (Command Line Interface) commands, which offer a powerful way to control these operations. However, one challenge we faced was that SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) doesn’t natively support calling CLI commands directly.

To overcome this limitation, we developed a custom solution: a FastAPI wrapper for the CLI commands, hosted on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). This API allows us to trigger SAP Datasphere task chains from SAC using a simple REST call. By utilizing SAC’s Multi Actions, we can now automate task chain execution seamlessly without leaving the SAC environment.

The following diagram illustrates the solution:

  1. SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) initiates a Multi Action.
  2. The Multi Action sends a REST call to the FastAPI CLI Wrapper hosted on SAP BTP.
  3. The FastAPI wrapper processes the request and triggers the Task Chain in SAP Datasphere.

This integration provides a seamless way to automate processes between SAC and SAP Datasphere, unlocking powerful automation capabilities within a familiar interface. In this blog, we’ll walk through the steps to implement this solution and highlight the benefits it brings to your data workflows.

CLI Setup

As a first step we created an OAuth client in SAP Datasphere.

And we also installed the datasphere CLI on our local machine.

Hint: For the CLI there exist already some great blogs and documentation:

  • Blogs: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-datasphere-cli-command-line-interface-for-sap-datasphere-overview/ba-p/13531596
  • CLI Documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/d0ecd6f297ac40249072a44df0549c1a/3f9a42ccde6b4b6aba121e2aab79c36d.html

Based on the following SAP Note https://me.sap.com/notes/3461484 we executed the login:
datasphere config cache init --host https://<tenantname>.hanacloudservices.cloud.sap/ --authorization-url https://<tenant name>.authentication.sap.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/authorize --token-url https://<tenant name>/oauth/token --client-id "<client id>" --client-secret "<client secret>"

After that you will be prompted to log on. Now you can display the secrets with the following command:
datasphere config secrets show --host "<your_host>”

This step is crucial as you'll need to save the secrets.json file for later use. Currently, the CLI requires user interaction for the initial login process. By completing this step and saving the secrets.json, you'll be able to automate future logins. The refresh_token validity, which is configured when creating the OAuth client, extends the duration before user re-authentication is needed. This allows for longer, uninterrupted use of the CLI without manual login. In the future, we hope that the log on process will no longer require user interaction, further streamlining automation workflows.

In this local CLI command you can now already test the different CLI commands.

API development and deployment

For the API development we created a BTP trial account (https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/hcp-create-trial-account..html). Here we navigate to our Business Application Studio and create a new dev space:

Here we select a “Full-Stack Application Using Productivity Tools” and the “Python-Tools”:

All of our code is available in the following Git repository: https://github.com/zpartner/cli_wrapper. This repository can give you an overview of how the application is structured and developed. For secrets management, we have provided template files that need to be renamed before use. Be sure to update the necessary authentication details in these templates to match your environment.

In the next section, the main points are described.

Key Points of the FastAPI Application

Authentication Handling:

  • The API uses authenticate_request(request) from auth.py to authenticate all requests across the endpoints, ensuring security.
  • Authentication is done at the start of every API route to verify the legitimacy of the requests.

Endpoints:

  • POST /: Simple authentication check. Returns a success message if authentication is successful.
  • /hello: Supports both GET and POST requests. Responds with a "Hello, World" message after authentication.
  • POST /run_task_chain: Executes a task chain based on provided space and object parameters. Returns a log ID after successful execution.
  • GET /get_logs: Retrieves logs for all runs of a specific task or task chain based on space and object.
  • GET /get_log_details: Retrieves detailed logs for a specific log ID with optional information levels (status or details).
  • GET /get_secrets/: Fetches the current secrets stored in secrets.json.
  • POST /update_secrets/: Allows updating or creating the secrets.json file by receiving a payload and writing it to disk.
  • GET /get_host/: Retrieves the current Datasphere host URL from the configuration.
  • POST /update_host/: Updates the Datasphere host URL.

Error Handling:

  • Uses HTTPException to handle and relay specific errors back to the client.
  • A general exception handler returns a 500 error for any unexpected issues.

Task Chain Execution:

  • The core functionality revolves around executing and retrieving logs for task chains, with endpoints like run_task_chain, get_logs, and get_log_details.

Configuration Management:

  • The API allows both getting and setting the Datasphere host using /get_host/ and /update_host/.
  • Secrets management is handled via endpoints to get and update secrets in secrets.json.

Key Points for Deployment (manifest.yaml)

Buildpacks:

  • The application requires both the Python and Node.js buildpacks. The python_buildpack is needed for the FastAPI app, while the nodejs_buildpack is necessary for npm to install and run the Datasphere CLI.

Command:

  • The command ensures that the necessary Node.js modules are installed, specifically @ash_G/datasphere-cli.
  • It also verifies the installation by printing the version of the Datasphere CLI.
  • Finally, the FastAPI server is started using uvicorn with --reload for hot-reloading, running on the default host and port specified by the cloud environment ($PORT).

Environment Variables:

  • NODE_ENV: Explicitly sets the NODE_ENV to development to prevent warnings.

Secrets management

The following .json files contain important information for our application:

  1. secrets.json: Contains the previously extracted information about the authentication
  2. config.json: Contains the Datasphere host URL, which the application uses to interact with the Datasphere environment.
  3. credentials.json: Stores user credentials (username and password hash) for basic authentication within the application.

Deployment

To deploy an application in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) via Cloud Foundry (CF) on SAP BTP, follow these steps:

  1. Login to Cloud Foundry:
    1. Open the terminal in BAS.
    2. Use the command: cf login -a “<API Endpoint>” and follow the instructions to log in. Please see below where to find your Cloud Foundry endpoin

  1. Deploy the Application
    1. use cf push in the app's directory.
  1. Check the application
    1. use cf apps to check the status of the application

After deployment you can also take a look at the generated documentation:

 

Testing with Postman

With Postman we can now make the first tests to interact with the API:

Integration with SAC

To interact now with API endpoints, we need to create a connection first in SAC. We go to connections and create a new HTTP connection. Also add the credentials for the Basic Authentication.

 

In the next step we create a Multi Action with an API step

In a story we can now integrate this Multi Action and execute it:

 

Back in the Datasphere we can see now the running instance, triggered from the SAC story.

Conclusion

In this project, we successfully integrated SAP Datasphere task chain automation with SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) by leveraging a FastAPI wrapper for CLI commands hosted on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). This integration significantly streamlined the process of automating task chains, allowing users to trigger SAP Datasphere operations seamlessly from within SAC. The use of SAC's Multi Actions feature combined with the FastAPI wrapper has enabled a more efficient and automated workflow, improving overall productivity.

Limitations

Despite the successful implementation, there are several limitations to consider:

  • Manual Logon Process: The initial logon process for the CLI still requires manual user interaction. Although subsequent logins can be automated using saved credentials (via the secrets.json file), this requirement introduces a hurdle to full automation.
  • Limited CLI Command Handling: The current implementation relies on a fixed set of CLI commands. Expanding the API to support additional commands may require further development and testing, especially when SAP Datasphere or SAC introduces new features.

Possible Challenges

  • API Maintenance: Regular updates to SAP Datasphere, SAC, or the FastAPI framework may require frequent maintenance of the custom API to ensure compatibility and stability.
  • Error Handling: While the API includes basic error handling, more comprehensive logging and troubleshooting mechanisms might be necessary to deal with complex errors or unexpected behaviors in production environments.
  • No GET Requests in SAC: SAC's current limitation of only supporting POST requests in Multi Actions means that no GET requests can be used. This restricts the ability to retrieve data or react dynamically based on the return of the API. As a result, the workflow is less flexible, as it cannot adapt based on the response from the API, which can limit the automation potential.
  • Inability to Work with POST Response Body in SAC: SAC is also currently unable to process or handle the body of a POST response. This further limits the interaction between SAC and the API, as you cannot use the data returned from the API to make decisions or trigger additional actions. This constraint makes it challenging to build more advanced workflows that rely on data returned from the API after a POST request.

Outlook

Looking forward, we hope SAP introduces a native API that can handle task chain automation without requiring custom development. This would eliminate the need for workarounds like FastAPI wrappers and enhance the native capabilities of SAC. With these improvements, SAP Datasphere and SAC integration could become even more seamless, offering more powerful automation without the need for custom solutions.

entry.title: 360° of SAP Analytics Cloud Performance Optimization
entry.content:
The importance of performance in reporting and analytics cannot be explained away. Efficient performance optimization in reporting ensures timely insights and enhances decision-making processes.  

To adequately address this crucial issue, we want to introduce our brand-new free learning journey, Managing Performance in SAP Analytics Cloud, designed to equip you with valuable skills in optimizing performance within SAP Analytics Cloud. 

This learning journey covers a comprehensive range of topics to elevate your performance optimization skills.  

  1. You will start by understanding the SAP Analytics Cloud architecture and its impact on performance. 
  2. Next, you will dive into performance troubleshooting using real-world scenarios and uncovering individual metrics to analyze performance.  
  3. The learning journey also includes an exploration of key tools used in performance analysis and measurement. 
  4. You will explore the relationship between story design and performance and dig deeper, designing performance optimized data visualization using pages and widgets.  
  5. You will use specific settings and features that will optimize performance during data loading. 
  6. Finally, you will explore scripting performance by analyzing the story script to identify and solve performance issues.  

We invite you to join this advanced learning journey and master the art of performance optimization in SAP Analytics Cloud. 

If you want to complement your performance skill set with an expert-guided hands-on session, explore our German SAP Data & Analytics Forum Agenda in November 2024.    

SAP Product Learning CoE Analytics

entry.title: Schedule publications SAC Stories in Excel file type
entry.content:
In SAP Analytics Cloud, you can now schedule story Table data in Excel format.  Here data would be exported in the Excel and delivered to the destination selected (email recipients).

Please note scheduling in the Excel format is available only while scheduling stories in Optimized View Mode or Story 2.0

When you are scheduling a publication in EXCEL format, you should select the Tables, whose data you wish to be exported in the Excel format. Within one publication (view) you can select maximum of five Tables, if you try to select more than five tables it will throw warning and will not allow you to create publication.

During selection it will list all the tables across different pages.
Schedule publication leverage manual export to Excel capabilities available the SAC stories at the table level, hence the data which you will get in the Excel using scheduling will be same as the one available in the manual export to EXCEL. It is good idea before scheduling, you can export the table data manually into EXCEL to validate your needs and review the data which would be exported during schedule time. Limitation available in the manual export to Excel will also be applicable in the schedule to Excel workflow.

While scheduling to EXCEL, the scope available is ALL only, point of view is not available.

Workflow
I have a story, who’s table data I would like to schedule in EXCEL format and deliverer to users over email.

 

Go to file and select schedule publication from the dropdown

 Fill all the required data for scheduling such as mode, name, recurrence etc and under file type select Excel

By default, the first exportable table would be selected but if you wish to change this, you can go to file setting and select the table whose data you wish to export to Excel in the scheduling workflow.

In my example, I have selected the 3 tables whose data would be exported and delivered in the scheduling workflow.

In the table listing page, you can see pages and tables are listed and the tables are listed with their respective titles. The Excel file generated and delivered will have name as provided in the file name section and tab name will be same as the Table name with in excel file. Please note for each SAC table there will be two tabs created; one which will have actual data and the other one will have the details about the table whose data will be exported.

 Name of the zip file could be provided using the file name.

 When the schedule run, the zip file with the excel file will be delivered over the email to the recipients.

entry.title: Seamless planning integration between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere - product FAQ
entry.content:
Introduction

This blogpost aims to give you an overview on the functionality offered by the seamless planning integration between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere. I already published a blogpost giving an overview of seamless planning and announcing the controlled release. It also covers the prerequisites for participation and usage of seamless planning. I recommend reading that one first. You can find it here.

Please, ask your questions in the comments and I will continuously amend and update this blogpost. Once the initial release comes closer, I will also provide you with an example use case.

 

What is seamless planning?

Please, first read here.

 

Connecting/linking SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere

What must I change in my tenant to use seamless planning?

Once seamless planning is available to you, the system owner of SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere needs to link the tenants. Linking will enable the selection of SAP Datasphere spaces as the data storage location for the supported object types.

What must customers know before linking SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere?

Besides the prerequisites listed under General Questions, SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere tenants can only be linked in a 1:1 relationship. One SAP Analytics Cloud tenant can be linked with only one Datasphere tenant. Customers must evaluate and carefully plan what this means for their specific landscape and lifecycle management.

In the future, we will aim to offer more flexibility regarding tenant relationships; however, there is no timeline for potential enrichment in this area.

 

Model deployment to SAP Datasphere

How do I deploy a model to SAP Datasphere?

Whenever you create a new model, you will be able to select the data storage location. This can be SAP Analytics Cloud (which means not using seamless planning) or SAP Datasphere (which means using seamless planning). If you choose SAP Datasphere, you need to choose a space.

You can also choose the data storage location when importing content via the Content Network in SAP Analytics Cloud.

Can I deploy Classic Account Models to SAP Datasphere?

No, Classic Account Models are not supported and will not be supported in the future.

Can I migrate existing models and content to the new architecture?

Migration support tooling is not available yet. This is considered a priority for future enhancements though.

What is the approach for public dimension tables and conversion tables?

When creating public dimension tables and conversion tables, you will also be prompted to select the data storage location. Public dimension tables’ and conversion tables’ data storage location must match the model’s data storage location to be used in the model.

 

Data management

How will data management be handled in a seamless planning setup?

With the initial release, data management will be handled using SAP Analytics Cloud’s data management capabilities, such as the data acquisition framework, data import service, and data export service. This means that Datasphere will not be allowed to write data directly into SAP Analytics Cloud’s tables using SAP Datasphere’s data integration tools, such as transformation flows. This is a potential future enhancement.

How will master data be managed between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere?

SAP Analytics Cloud will continue using SAP Analytics Cloud managed dimensions to store and model master data required for planning. In the future, we want to allow SAP Analytics Cloud to consume dimensions previously created in SAP Datasphere.

 

Consumption in SAP Datasphere

How are SAP Analytics Cloud artefacts exposed and consumed in SAP Datasphere?

For the model’s fact table and public dimension tables, you can choose to expose them in the data builder.

SAP Analytics Cloud models will expose the underlying data foundation as a “Local Table (Fact)”, while the public dimension tables will expose the master data as a “Local Table (Dimension)” associated with a translation table (storing the multi-language descriptions) and, in future, hierarchy tables. Then, SAP Datasphere can use SAP Analytics Cloud-exposed objects in graphical views, SQL views, analytics models, transformation flows, etc.

Can SAP Analytics Cloud objects be changed directly in SAP Datasphere?

SAP Analytics Cloud objects will appear in read-only mode in SAP Datasphere, meaning that SAP Datasphere modelers cannot make structural changes to these objects.

Can planning data be shared outside the space in SAP Datasphere?

Once SAP Analytics Cloud’s models and public dimensions are exposed in SAP Datasphere, SAP Datasphere modelers can choose to share these objects with other spaces using SAP Datasphere’s sharing functionality.

Can I monitor the effect of my planning models and activities on my SAP Datasphere tenant?

With seamless planning, planning models are deployed to SAP Datasphere spaces. As such, they consume disk and memory. Also, planning activities like publishing, running data actions etc. are running on SAP Datasphere’s database. You can use SAP Datasphere's system monitors to track these loads.

 

Planning Activities

Can I change plan data directly in SAP Datasphere?

SAP Analytics Cloud application interfaces will remain in control of the model structure and all data changes. Direct write access to the planning fact table via SAP Datasphere will not be possible.  However, we consider a number of future enhancements to consume data from SAP Datasphere in the planning model.   

Could Datasphere extend SAP Analytics Cloud planning capabilities by accessing libraries of algorithms from Python, Azure, Pandas, etc.?

Programming languages like Python can extend Datasphere in many areas, including ETL, reporting, and analysis, and provide access to libraries like Pandas and NumPy. A good overview can be found here. Additionally, this blogpost shows how to implement a SAP Business AI project with a time-series forecast by using the embedded Predictive Analysis Library.

 

Access, roles, security

What roles are required for the users?

Let’s look at this from different angles.

  • Modelers store the model in SAP Datasphere and build SAP Datasphere assets on top:
    • SAP Analytics Cloud Modeler and SAP Datasphere DW Modeler or equivalent role to create models and modeling assets in SAP Analytics Cloud, and save them in an SAP Datasphere space, with Read & Create permission on the SAP Datasphere data builder.
    • SAP Datasphere DW Modeler role or equivalent privileges to create views and analytical models in SAP Datasphere.
    • SAP Datasphere DW Consumer role or equivalent to view objects in spaces.

 

  • Planners need to have a user in SAP Datasphere. Besides that, they require the known roles and data access in SAP Analytics Cloud (Data Access Control or roles; no change in the context of seamless planning).

 

  • Reporting on top of analytic models is secured via SAP Datasphere, i.e. Read access and SAP Datasphere Data Access Control. Besides that, they require the known roles in SAP Analytics Cloud to see stories. This has not changed with seamless planning.

How is data authorization for the planning model handled?

For the planning model, data access control and roles/model data privacy remain the tools to secure data.

A harmonization is a potential future enhancement.

Will there be common user management across SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere?

Currently, no common user management is planned. To streamline the user management process based on the existing setup, customers could configure a custom central SAML identity provider to propagate user changes across SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere. More info can be found here.

 

Restrictions

Are there any feature imparities when using seamless planning?

Note the following feature imparities compared to the features offered for models that are not using seamless planning (as of QRC4.2024). We will try to lift them asap:

  • Delta subscriptions for Data Export Service are unavailable (planned)
  • New member selector does not appear for any object on top of seamless planning models (planned Q1)
  • Restrictions in geo maps (planned):
    • Geo areas are not visible on the geo map
    • Geo points that should be displayed in the choropleth layer of the geo map are not displayed
  • Import packages in Content Network cannot be repointed to an SAP Datasphere space if they contain Data Analyzer artefacts (planned Q1)

 

What are important functional restrictions in the current scope of seamless planning?

I’d like to point out these functional restrictions that we want to remove in releases after the controlled release:  

  • Having shared public dimensions across models is only possible when the models and the public dimensions are deployed to the same SAP Datasphere space. The same applies to the currency rate tables.
  • Hierarchies are not exposed to SAP Datasphere. If they are needed, SAP Datasphere modelers are required to rebuild them.
  • Cross-model planning activities, such as data action cross-model copy step, are only allowed if the required models are deployed to the same data persistence (i.e. all in SAP Analytics Cloud or all in SAP Datasphere), and in the case of objects deployed to SAP Datasphere, to the same SAP Datasphere space.

Are there features that will not be supported on seamless planning models?

Indeed, there are features that will not be supported for Seamless Planning models. There are no plans to support them in the future as they are being phased out of SAP Analytics Cloud:

  • Classis Design Experience. You can only consume Seamless Planning models in Optimized Design Experience.
  • Input Tasks. Use the SAP Analytics Cloud Calendar instead to coordinate your planning activities.

 

Future scope

What features are planned for the future?

First and foremost, we want to remove the functional restrictions outlined above. Then, we plan highly value-adding features to strengthen the cross-consumption of data, streamline modeling efforts and the orchestration of seamless planning use cases. We have many ideas to strengthen the integration even more. These are just some of them:

  • (Live) Cross-consumption of SAP Datasphere data
  • Migration support for existing SAP Analytics Cloud content to the new architecture
  • Re-use of dimensions from SAP Datasphere
  • Cross-orchestration of workflows between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere with multi actions and task chains

 

Licensing

What licenses are required to use seamless planning?

You need to own licenses for both SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere. SAP Analytics Cloud is predominantly licensed via user-based model whereas SAP Datasphere is licensed by capacity. In the seamless planning scenario, you would license the planning functionality via SAP Analytics Cloud users and license the hardware (storage, memory, compute) required for planning via SAP Datasphere capacity units. 

 

Conclusion

Seamless planning is a big change and a big opportunity. Take the time to think about what it can mean for you and let us know your questions in the comments! As said, I will continuously amend and update this blogpost.

entry.title: Seamless planning between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere is coming! Controlled release in Q4
entry.content:
Introduction

You may have heard of it already. We have been busy bringing the integration between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere for planning use case to an entirely new level. And we are about to reach a crucial milestone as we are going for a controlled release with QRC4.2024 for SAP Analytics Cloud running on HANA Cloud infrastructure.

In this blogpost, I want to give you an overview of the concept of seamless planning, the current scope, future plans, prerequisites and release plans. More info is available in my second blogpost on the topic where we cover more in-depth product FAQ.

 

Seamless Planning – Overview and Functionality

What is seamless planning?

Seamless planning is a new approach to the planning integration between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere. It is not a new product. With seamless planning, you can store the SAP Analytics Cloud model’s data (fact and master data) directly in SAP Datasphere, reducing the data footprint and making it available for real-time consumption, additional processing and extensibility scenarios using SAP Datasphere’s capabilities.

SAP Analytics Cloud will continue to define the model structure, own the model’s metadata, define calculations, planning processes, etc. SAP Analytics Cloud modelers can opt for SAP Datasphere data storage when creating new models and public dimension tables, or when importing content. They can also control the visibility of the SAP Analytics Cloud objects in SAP Datasphere. These objects are read-only in SAP Datasphere.

The goal is to reduce the time to action and foster collaboration across different lines of business by enabling planning and analytics on a simplified, centralized, and governed data platform. Hereby, we are facilitating true extend planning & analysis (xP&A).

What is in the controlled release? (incl. limitations)

In the first release of seamless planning, you will be able to select an SAP Datasphere space as the storage location for your planning model. This is a per-model choice. Within the model, you can then choose to expose fact data and/or distinct public dimension tables in the data builder in SAP Datasphere. You can also import content using the Content Network and deploy data to SAP Datasphere.                      

With that, you will be able to realize the following benefits:

  • Reduced data redundancy
  • Enhanced flexibility to perform live reporting across SAP Analytics Cloud and other data sources based on joint SAP Datasphere analytic models
  • Extensibility of SAP Analytics Cloud planning using SAP Datasphere capabilities such as data operations using SQL editor. Note, programming languages like Python can extend Datasphere in many areas as well.

Note the following feature imparities during controlled release compared to the features offered for models that are not using seamless planning. We will try to lift them asap:

  • Delta subscriptions for Data Export Service are unavailable (planned)
  • New member selector does not appear for any object on top of seamless planning models (planned Q1)
  • Restrictions in geo maps (planned):
    • Geo areas are not visible on the geo map
    • Geo points that should be displayed in the choropleth layer of the geo map are not displayed
  • Import packages in Content Network cannot be repointed to an SAP Datasphere space if they contain Data Analyzer artefacts (planned Q1)

Also, I’d like to point out some more functional restrictions that we want to remove in releases after the controlled release:  

  • Having shared public dimensions across models is only possible when the models and the public dimensions are deployed to the same SAP Datasphere space. The same applies to the currency rate tables.
  • Hierarchies are not exposed to SAP Datasphere. If they are needed, SAP Datasphere modelers are required to rebuild them.
  • Cross-model planning activities, such as data action cross-model copy step, are only allowed if the required models are deployed to the same data persistence (i.e. all in SAP Analytics Cloud or all in SAP Datasphere), and in the case of objects deployed to SAP Datasphere, to the same SAP Datasphere space.

What features are planned for the future?

First and foremost, we want to remove the limitations outlined above. Then, we plan highly value-adding features to strengthen the cross-consumption of data, streamline modeling efforts and the orchestration of seamless planning use cases. We have many ideas to strengthen the integration even more. These are just some of them:

  • (Live) Cross-consumption of SAP Datasphere data
  • Migration support for existing SAP Analytics Cloud content to the new architecture
  • Re-use of dimensions from SAP Datasphere
  • Cross-orchestration of workflows between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere with multi actions and task chains

 

Infrastructure & prerequisites

What are the prerequisites to use seamless planning?

To connect SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere for seamless planning, the following prerequisites apply:

  • SAP Analytics Cloud running on HANA Cloud
  • SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere are located in the same data center. SAP datacenters are not in scope
  • SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere use the same identity provider
  • SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere tenants are linked for seamless planning in a 1:1 relationship by the system owner

How can I learn more about the HANA Cloud migration?

Learn more in this blogpost.

 

Controlled release & way forward

Who can participate in the Controlled Release?

Obviously, you must be a customer of both SAP Analytics Cloud planning and SAP Datasphere. Your SAP Analytics Cloud tenant must run on HANA Cloud (see above).

Is there a way to participate in the Controlled Release if my SAP Analytics Cloud tenant is not running on HANA Cloud?

The only way to participate if your SAP Analytics Cloud tenant is not running on HANA Cloud is the purchase of a new SAP Analytics Cloud tenant. However, the deployment of any new tenant will be aligned with your existing tenants. So, it must be ensured that it will be deployed on HANA Cloud instead. To do so, you can use a BTPEA agreement or you work with your account team to flag the delivery of the tenant as an exception with our operations teams.

Be aware that moving content built on new tenants to your old tenants once these are migrated is challenging. This is due to the 1:1 relationship between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere, the redundancy of objects deployed in different ways in the tenants and built content in SAP Datasphere. This is why the approach of a new SAP Analytics Cloud tenant on HANA Cloud for the Controlled Release is recommended for POC purposes only.

How can I sign up?

A registration page is available on Customer Influence. Link

When can I expect general availability?

General availability is planned for QRC1.2025. Note that availability only applies to SAP Analytics Cloud tenants running on HANA Cloud. Refer to this blogpost for more info on migration plans.

 

Conclusion

The upcoming controlled release of seamless planning marks a milestone in the journey to true extended planning & analysis (xP&A). Plan data now lives right next to actual data. Extend the capabilities of SAP Analytics Cloud using SAP Datasphere’s capabilities. In the future, you shall exchange data across planning domains seamlessly. You shall see modeling and data management efforts become streamlined and efficient.

We are looking forward to welcoming many customers to this controlled release and to making seamless planning available across our customer base in 2025. We shall deliver additional functionality unlocking more of the potential of this incredibly powerful set-up.

entry.title: Deep Insight of Your Master Data with MDG Data Quality Analytics 2.0
entry.content:
Abstract

SAP Master Data Governance is a state-of-the-art master data management application. Data Quality Management in MDG enables you to define master data quality rules, striving for consistent usage across all points of entry and enabling data quality monitoring and remediation.

On release SAP S/4HANA 2023FSP2 and Cloud 2408, SAP Master Data Governance has delivered data quality analytics 2.0, which enables end users, from Chief Data Officer to master data specialist, a real time quality abstract, and the data quality drill down further by important segment e.g., rule attribute and important business partner/customer/suppliers/products attributes and organization unit for example Country, Plant, Sales Organization,  Company Codes etc..  

It includes mainly:

  • 14 Analytics CDS views for product and business partners
  • 2 Out-of-box data quality dashboards for product and business partners in SAP Analytics Cloud by leveraging these analytics CDS views.

Highlights:

Data Quality Analytics 2.0 provides Quality Score on dimensions for example, master data  attribute, organization unit etc., used in the dashboard. It gives you a clear comparison between organization units for example plant, company code etc., on same data quality standard. Each Plant, Sales Org, Region owner have clear picture of their own data quality status and ranking among all organizations. From there, they are motivated to identify a problem, dig root cause, and finally put it into action to improve data quality.  From aggregated data quality score, it is also possible to drill-down into each erroneous master data to trigger data remediation process.

 

Analytics CDS Views

See 7 analytics CDS views for Business Partners and 7 analytics CDS views for Product  in SAP Help Portal 

All these analytics CDS views support custom fields extensibility on business partner, customer, suppliers, and products master data. 

In S/4,  end user can open view data of analytics CDS in graphic or reporting UI using Query Browser app. Key user can also configure smart business apps for multiple end users using Manage KPI and Report app.

Query Brower - MDG Data Quality CDS ViewsQuery Browser - Customer Sales Data Quality Analysis

Understand the Quality Score

Each analytics query CDS view provides a measure Quality Score, which is calculated dynamically for your selection dimension attribute.

The Quality Score give you clear comparison between organization units e.g., plant, company code on same data quality standard.  Each Country, Plant, Sales Org, Region owner have clear picture of their own data quality status and ranking among all organizations.

Data Quality Score by Country/Region

 

Data Quality Dashboards 2.0 in SAP Analytics Cloud

The 2 dashboards provide a deep quality analytics for business partner(including customer and supplier)  and product master data which can quickly identify the root cause of quality problem and continuously monitoring data health to safeguard customer core business. It enables end users, from Chief Data Officer to master data specialist, a real time quality abstract, and the data quality drill down further by important segment e.g., rule attribute and important business partner/customer/suppliers/products attributes and organization unit for example Country, Plant, Sales Organization,  Company Codes etc.  

Both Dashboard contains an Overview page providing data quality score on following reporting: 

  • Dimension Category 
  • Dimension 
  • Validation Rule 

Dashboard for Business Partner( SAP__MDG_DQ_BP_V2)  contains an Analytics page providing quality score and number of Not OK evaluation results for following data: 

  • Business Partner General Data 
  • Customer General Data 
  • Customer Sales Data 
  • Customer Company Code Data 
  • Supplier General Data 
  • Supplier Purchasing Data 
  • Supplier Company Code Data 

Dashboard for Products( SAP__MDG_DQ_PRODUCT_V2)  contains an Analytics page providing quality score and number of Not OK evaluation results for following data: 

  • Product Header data 
  • Product Plant Data 
  • Product Sales Data 
  • Product Description 
  • Product Valuation Data 
  • Product Classification Data 
  • Product Dimensions Data 

Both dashboards provide embedded authorization control via live data connection of analytics CDS views in MDG on S/4. And also provides integration to MDG system for data remediation seamlessly. 

SAP Analytics Cloud - Data Quality Dashboard 2.0 - BP: Overview

SAP Analytics Cloud - Data Quality Dashboard 2.0 - BP: Analysis

 

SAP Analytics Cloud - Data Quality Dashboard 2.0 - Product: Overview

SAP Analytics Cloud - Data Quality Dashboard 2.0 - Product: Analysis

Set Up Dashboard for Your SAC Tenant

Open Content Network from your tenant, find the SAP Master Data Governance – Data Quality Analysis package, and import it.

You can find details in my previous blog:  Get Your Master Data Quality Insight from SAP **bleep**... - SAP Community

SAP Analytics Cloud - Business Content Network


 

Summary

Following this blog, you should be able to

  • explain what’s provided functions in MDG Data Quality Analytics 2.0.
  • set up the out-of-box data quality dashboard in SAP Analytics Cloud for your end user or using S/4 embedded analytics capabilities.

entry.title: 🚀Fire of Innovations: Highlights from SAP TechEd 2024 📢
entry.content:
The SAP TechEd 2024 event, themed “Fire of Innovations,” has once again set the stage for groundbreaking advancements in enterprise technology. Held virtually on October 8-9, this year’s conference showcased a plethora of new tools, updates, and strategies designed to empower businesses and developers alike. Here’s a deep dive into some of the most exciting announcements and innovations from the event.

1. Joule: The AI Copilot Revolution

One of the standout highlights was the unveiling of new capabilities for Joule, SAP’s generative AI copilot. Joule has been supercharged with collaborative AI agents that can perform complex, cross-disciplinary tasks. These agents are designed to break down silos within organizations, enhancing productivity and allowing human workers to focus on more creative and strategic activities.

For instance, in dispute management, autonomous AI agents can now analyze and resolve issues such as incorrect invoices or duplicate payments. In financial accounting, these agents streamline processes like bill payments and ledger updates, quickly addressing any inconsistencies.

2. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Enhancements

SAP continues to expand its Business Technology Platform (BTP), which is central to its cloud strategy. This year, SAP announced the expansion of BTP coverage through eight new SAP-managed data centers. Additionally, the SAP Discovery Center now offers a Quick-Account-Setup feature, enabling businesses to deploy specific use cases with just a click.

The integration of ABAP Cloud into SAP Build was another significant update. This integration allows developers to create and maintain ABAP Cloud projects directly from the SAP Build lobby, ensuring a seamless and efficient development process.

3. Innovations in SAP Build

SAP Build has been strengthened to support a “Clean Core” strategy, which is crucial for maintaining system consistency and upgradeability. The SAP Build Extensibility Wizard, introduced for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, helps both key users and developers create on-stack and side-by-side extensions in an embedded experience.

4. Harnessing the Power of Data with SAP Knowledge Graph

SAP introduced the SAP Knowledge Graph, a next-generation solution that connects SAP data with rich business context. This tool is poised to help developers unlock the full value of SAP data, driving more informed decision-making and innovation.

5. Global Reach and Accessibility

This year’s SAP TechEd was a truly global event, with a free two-day virtual format that included keynotes, technical sessions, and developer enablement activities. The event was designed to be accessible to a worldwide audience, ensuring that everyone could benefit from the latest SAP innovations.

Conclusion

SAP TechEd 2024 has once again demonstrated SAP’s commitment to driving innovation and empowering businesses with cutting-edge technology. From AI advancements with Joule to enhancements in SAP BTP and SAP Build, the “Fire of Innovations” theme truly encapsulates the transformative potential of these new tools and strategies. As businesses continue to navigate the complexities of the digital age, SAP’s innovations provide a robust foundation for growth and success.

Stay tuned for more updates and insights from SAP TechEd as we continue to explore the future of enterprise technology.

Feel free to share your thoughts or ask any questions about the event! What innovation are you most excited about?

entry.title: Maximizing M&A Success: How SAP Solutions Drive Growth and Effectiveness
entry.content:
Maximizing M&A Success:  How SAP Solutions Drive Growth and Effectiveness

Authored by:  Christina Grace, SAP Global Finance Value Advisor & Marie Trojani, SAP Global HCM Value Advisor

Mergers and acquisitions (M&A) are complex transactions that can significantly alter the trajectory of a business. While they present opportunities for growth, diversification, and enhanced market presence, they also come with inherent challenges that can hinder success. One critical factor in navigating these challenges is the effective integration of systems, processes, people, and cultures. SAP offers a robust framework that can significantly enhance a company’s effectiveness for companies pursuing an M&A strategy. This blog explores how SAP facilitates successful M&A outcomes in the areas of Finance and HR and discusses potential inflection points in the journey to ensure success.

Understanding the M&A Landscape

Before delving into how SAP can assist during the M&A process, it's important to understand the typical M&A landscape. The process generally involves several phases:

  1. Planning and Strategy Development: Identifying potential targets and assessing strategic fit. This includes an assessment of current systems, people, and processes in both organizations. The goal should be to identify gaps and overlaps that could impact integration.  This phase also involves engaging key stakeholders from both organizations to ensure buy-in and prepare teams for the upcoming changes.
  2. Due Diligence: Analyzing financials, operations, and risks associated with the target company.  This analysis should provide insights into the target company’s strengths and weaknesses.  During this phase, data management tools can be used to perform scenario modeling of the two companies, set KPI goals, measure compliance, and ensure data harmonization.  A risk assessment should be performed to evaluate potential risks associated with the acquisition. Identifying risks early allows for proactive management strategies. That’s also at this stage that you can evaluate the cultural fit between the two companies.
  3. Integration and Strategy Management: Merging systems, processes, people, and cultures post-acquisition. Develop a comprehensive integration roadmap that outlines timelines, responsibilities, and key milestones. This roadmap should leverage GenAI integration capabilities to ensure a seamless transition. Prepare a training and support plan to educate employees about new systems and processes. Effective training will minimize disruptions and foster a sense of ownership among staff.  Real-time monitoring of the integration process is essential and allows for quick adjustments if any issues arise.  Establish feedback mechanisms to gather input from employees about the integration process. Utilize these insights to refine strategies and address concerns promptly. Particularly, HR concerns can appear quickly around job redundancies, salary gaps, talent retention for critical roles and employee morale.
  4. Post-Merger Evaluation: Assessing the success of the integration and overall M&A performance. After integration, evaluate the performance of the combined entity using advanced analytics tools. Assess whether M&A goals have been met and identify areas for further improvement, realizing greater synergies, and operating more efficiently.  Perform a cultural integration review by assessing employee engagement and satisfaction.  This allows companies to gauge the success of cultural integration, to gather insights, and to address any lingering issues.

Each phase presents unique challenges that SAP addresses with agility, transparency, and scalability.

The Role of SAP in M&A

SAP provides a comprehensive suite of integrated solutions designed to enhance operational efficiency, drive revenue growth, harmonize data, and improve real-time insights and overall business intelligence. Here’s how SAP Finance and HR solutions can specifically help companies pursuing an M&A strategy:

Seamless Integration of Systems

Challenge: One of the biggest hurdles in M&A is integrating disparate IT systems from merging organizations. Incompatible systems can lead to inefficiencies, data silos, and disruptions in business operations.

SAP Solution: SAP’s integrated platform enables organizations to unify disparate systems under one umbrella. With tools such as SAP S/4HANA, businesses can achieve real-time data integration and consistency, allowing for streamlined operations across the newly formed entity.  In the HR community, harmonizing different payroll, benefits, and performance management systems requires strategic planning and meticulous execution to avoid disruptions and maintain employee satisfaction.  This seamless integration reduces downtime and ensures continuity of service, which is critical during transitional phases.

Efficient Data Management

Challenge: During an M&A integration, organizations face an overwhelming amount of data that needs to be migrated, analyzed, and integrated. Poor data management can result in costly mistakes and compliance issues.

SAP Solution: SAP’s data management tools, including SAP Business Technology Platform, provide robust capabilities for data migration, cleansing, and integration. These solutions ensure that data from both companies are harmonized and accessible, allowing decision-makers to derive insights quickly and effectively. Real-time analytics further enable businesses to identify trends and make informed decisions swiftly.

Streamlined Financial Reporting

Challenge: Accurate financial reporting is essential during M&A, both for compliance and for assessing the financial health of the combined entity. However, multiple financial systems and reporting standards can complicate this process.

SAP Solution: SAP solutions offer integrated financial management tools that facilitate streamlined reporting and compliance with international financial standards. With SAP Analytics Cloud, businesses can create real-time financial dashboards, enabling stakeholders to monitor KPIs and other financial metrics seamlessly. This capability is crucial for transparent communication with investors, internal stakeholders, and regulatory bodies.

Enhanced Operational Efficiency

Challenge: Post-M&A, organizations often encounter operational redundancies and inefficiencies due to overlapping processes and systems.  Furthermore, redundancies and restructuring can lead to workforce reductions, which may result in the loss of valuable talent and institutional knowledge.

SAP Solution: SAP’s process automation capabilities can help identify and eliminate redundancies. For example, with SAP Ariba, companies can enhance procurement processes, while SAP SuccessFactors can streamline human resources management. HR must manage these transitions delicately, ensuring fair and compliant processes while maintaining operational continuity. These solutions foster operational efficiencies that lead to cost savings and improved productivity across the organization. 

Change Management Support

Challenge: M&A transactions often lead to significant challenges to company culture, processes, and personnel. Concerns about the unknown and resistance to change can derail integration efforts. 

SAP Solution:  Fostering employee engagement and loyalty can be key to overcoming these obstacles. Initiatives to support change management, such as training programs and team-building activities, are essential but must be carefully tailored to address the specific needs and concerns of the workforce.  SAP offers tools to support change management, including SAP Enable Now, which provides training and support resources for employees.  This tool allows organizations to create custom training materials and resources that can facilitate a smoother transition. By preparing employees for the changes ahead, companies can mitigate resistance and foster a more collaborative environment.

Risk Management and Compliance

Challenge: M&A transactions are inherently risky, and the complexities of integrating different compliance standards can lead to potential legal and regulatory issues.

SAP Solution: SAP’s risk management tools, such as SAP GRC (Governance, Risk, and Compliance), enable businesses to assess and manage risks effectively. These solutions provide a framework for identifying compliance gaps and ensuring adherence to regulatory requirements, thereby protecting the organization from potential liabilities.

Cultural Integration

Challenge: Merging different company cultures can be one of the most challenging aspects of M&A. Misalignment can lead to employee dissatisfaction and turnover.  M&A often herald significant organizational transformation, presenting numerous challenges for HR and employees. Merging distinct corporate cultures can lead to misalignment, confusion, and conflicts. HR must navigate the complex task of creating a cohesive environment where employees from both organizations feel valued and understood.

SAP Solution: While SAP solutions primarily focus on operational aspects, they can also support cultural integration indirectly. Tools like SAP SuccessFactors provide insights into employee engagement and performance, enabling leadership to address cultural challenges proactively. By fostering open communication and collaboration through these platforms, organizations can work towards aligning their cultures more effectively.

Job Security

Challenge: Another critical issue is job security. Employees often face anxiety and uncertainty about their future roles, leading to decreased morale and productivity. There is a higher risk for top talent to leave the company during times of uncertainty.

SAP Solution: Transparent communication from HR is vital to mitigate these concerns, as well as a comprehensive view into employee data such as competencies and job profiles. Analytics and AI can facilitate informed decisions and proactively alert if there are potential risks. Yet finding the balance between transparency and confidentiality can be challenging.

In navigating all of these challenges, Finance, HR and IT all play a crucial role in facilitating a smooth transition, fostering a positive organizational culture, and maintaining employee well-being and productivity during M&A activities.

Benefits of SAP Solutions in M&A

  • Efficiency: Streamlines operations and reduces integration time
  • Cost Savings: Minimizes redundancies and operational costs
  • Real-Time Insights: Provides data-driven decision-making capabilities
  • Risk Mitigation: Enhances compliance and governance
  • Employee Engagement: Supports smooth cultural integration through training and engagement tools

Conclusion

In today’s fast-paced business environment, the success of M&A hinges on effective integration and management of systems, people, data, and processes. In an era where adaptability and efficiency are paramount, embracing SAP Finance and HR solutions not only supports immediate M&A goals but also lays a solid foundation for sustained success in an ever-evolving marketplace.

SAP offers a comprehensive toolkit for companies navigating the multifaceted complexities and challenges of M&A, enabling organizations to streamline operations, enhance decision-making, and drive overall success.  By leveraging SAP’s integrated tools, companies can navigate the complexities of M&A with greater confidence and effectiveness. By facilitating integration, enhancing data management, and supporting operational efficiency, SAP helps organizations achieve their M&A objectives while laying the groundwork for long-term success.  As organizations continue to pursue growth through acquisitions, those that adopt SAP solutions will be well-positioned to realize the full potential of their M&A strategies, fostering a more agile and competitive future.

entry.title: SAP Datasphere - How to flip sign the Measure value by GL Account type like SAP Analytics Cloud.
entry.content:
Before we start discussing, I would like to let you know that you can get a 30-day trial instance of SAP Datasphere and access basic SAP Datasphere trainings here.  https://developers.sap.com/tutorial-navigator.html

As an SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) consultant, I am currently working for a customer who wants to use SAP Datasphere as a reporting data source for SAC. This will allow them to integrate SAC Planning data, Group Reporting Consolidated Actual data, and third-party data.

This is a common scenario, but the main problem is customer cannot figure out how to flip sign based on the GL Account type in SAP Datasphere. Additionally, GL Accounts usually have a hierarchical structure like Balance Sheet (B/S) and Profit & Loss Statement (P/L). Therefore, after the value is aggregated, it should be displayed with the correct sign.

In financial systems, Income and Liability accounts data are usually stored in the database with opposite signs, while Expense and Asset accounts data are stored as they are.

For example, if Revenue is 1000 and Expense is 300, the Net Revenue will be 700.

To achieve this, Revenue is stored as -1000 and Expense as 300. When added to together, the result is -700. Since the Net Revenue account is an Income Account, the Financial system will flip its sign before displaying it to users, showing Net Revenue as 700. 

This behavior is consistent across all SAP systems (ERP, BW, BPC and SAC.) 

Unfortunately, DataSphere does not yet provide this feature. I believe the SAP Datasphere development team is considering it, but we need a solution for financial reporting in the meantime.

The solution is....

'Create a calculated measure in the Analytic Model using a CASE WHEN Statement'.

Let's Assume, we have an attribute in the ACCT dimension called Actype, which has values 'INC', 'EXP,' AST and 'LEQ'. The measure name is Amount.

By creating a calculated measure called 'CM SIGNDATA' as shown below, we can display the correct sign data. The Amount measure shows the same sign as it is stored in the database.

CASE
    WHEN Actype_ACCT = 'INC’ THEN Amount * (-1)
    WHEN Actype_ACCT = 'LEQ’ THEN Amount * (-1)
    ELSE
          Amount
END

I hope SAP Datasphere supports this as built-in feature like SAC so that we don't need to create additional calculated Measure.

NOTE:  you can find Actype_ACCT attribute when you create the calculated measure. After selecting 'Dimension' and searching 'ACCT', you will find it. just click it then it will be inserted in the formula without typing.

I hope this article helps anyone facing similar issues.

entry.title: SAP TechEd 2024 - Summary
entry.content:
