SAP TechEd 2024 delivered an inspiring keynote with a strong focus on how technology drives real-world business innovations. This session brought to light the latest advances in AI, cloud solutions, and SAP's vision for the future of enterprise software.

The keynote opened with a thought-provoking introduction about the human brain, its complexities, and how innovation emerges from millions of connections forming new ideas. This metaphor seamlessly tied into SAP's philosophy of turning ideas into tangible solutions—where concepts become tools for solving real business problems.

Innovation at the Heart of SAP’s Strategy

One of the central themes was the growing importance of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in SAP’s offerings. AI is now deeply integrated into SAP’s cloud-driven portfolio, designed to simplify customers' lives and meet evolving business needs. Senior AI Scientists at SAP are working on harnessing AI to make business software smarter and more intuitive. The keynote also emphasized SAP's efforts to ensure that its software is configurable, extensible, and unified, providing a seamless suite-like experience across its platforms.

SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)

A significant highlight was SAP's Business Technology Platform (BTP), which has seen rapid growth in customer adoption. BTP is the cornerstone for SAP’s cloud and AI-driven future, empowering businesses with application development, automation, data analytics, and AI capabilities all within one unified platform. The keynote highlighted that over 27,000 live customers and 2,800 partners are leveraging SAP BTP to drive innovation.

The announcement of new BTP regions running on infrastructure from partners like AWS, Microsoft, and Google, along with SAP-managed data centers, shows SAP’s commitment to providing broader regional coverage and reliable service. This expansion aims to help businesses operate globally while maintaining efficiency, security, and compliance.

Enhancements in Application Development and Automation

SAP unveiled its Clean Core Strategy, which allows businesses to build custom extensions in a way that keeps the core ERP system maintainable and upgradeable. With SAP’s low-code environment, developers can create AI-powered applications with ease, while ABAP Cloud now supports both on-stack and side-by-side development, giving businesses more flexibility in their customizations.

One exciting feature introduced was the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard, a tool designed to streamline the process of creating custom fields and extensions, allowing users to enhance SAP applications with minimal coding.

SAP Build and GenAI

In keeping with the AI theme, SAP showcased the GenAI capabilities within SAP Build. With Joule, an AI assistant, developers can create complex data models, build extensions, and generate applications through natural language prompts. This AI-driven approach reduces manual coding and helps users integrate advanced functionalities like time series forecasting and predictive analytics into their business processes.

Data and Analytics Innovations

The keynote further highlighted innovations in data and analytics, specifically focusing on SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Analytics Cloud, and SAP Datasphere. The HANA Cloud Vector Engine and Knowledge Graph Engine were introduced, both of which enable businesses to harness structured and unstructured data for more context-aware AI-driven decisions. These engines promise faster and more accurate data processing, particularly in scenarios involving large language models and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).

Additionally, the keynote showcased how SAP Datasphere is being adopted by large enterprises like Hershey’s and Bosch for data cataloging, integration, and self-service analytics, empowering businesses to access trusted data with ease.

Integration Suite and AI-Driven Optimization

The SAP Integration Suite now offers a modern, AI-powered solution for real-time end-to-end business process integration. With thousands of pre-built integrations and connectors, businesses can seamlessly integrate SAP and third-party applications. SAP is also leveraging AI-generated recommendations to optimize custom scripts, further enhancing developer productivity and reducing memory consumption.

Conclusion

SAP TechEd 2024 demonstrated SAP’s unwavering commitment to making innovation accessible, impactful, and transformative. From the integration of advanced AI capabilities to the expansion of BTP, SAP is setting a new standard in enterprise software. Whether you are looking to enhance your application development, automate processes, or harness the power of data, SAP’s latest innovations offer powerful tools to bring your ideas to life.

As SAP continues to evolve, the future of enterprise technology is more connected, intelligent, and adaptable than ever before.

Here is the Keynote from SAP:

Comment below your thoughts on TechEd.

entry.title: SAP Developer News: TechEd Day 2
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

SAP TechEd General

  • News Guide: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/news-guide.html
  • TechEd Watch Live: https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html

Data and Analytics

  • Data and Analytics News: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/news-guide/data-and-analytics.html
  • SAP HANA Cloud Sessions: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog?tab.day=20241009&search.offeringsproducts=1693018299239028TpZb

Integration

  • Integration News: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/news-guide/integration.html
  • Integration Sessions: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog?tab.day=20241009&search.track=1692371026432005VcKa

New SAP Build Capabilities

  • SAP Build Extensibility Wizard: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-build-extensibility-wizard-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/ba-p/13867427
  • AD105 | Enhance your fusion team collaboration with ABAP Cloud and SAP Build https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog/session/1721791179884001rYRe
  • AD200 | Extend Joule with SAP Build and develop custom enterprise skills https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog/session/1722394882377001d2M9

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 TechEd Intro
1:13 Data and Analytics
2:25 Integration
3:49 New SAP Build Capabilities

Transcript

[Intro] It's a long way from Philly to Heidelberg, but the show must go on. We're late, aren't we? Yeah, we're late. We all know there's nothing like the real thing. Luckily, we've got our best people on the job. Oh, sorry, Josh! Josh, if you pulled your own weight, we wouldn't have to carry you through this place.

[Witalij] There were many extremely interesting announcements related to data and analytics during SAP TechEd Virtual. Some of them are coming in Q1 of 2025, while others are already available. For example, you might have noticed that as technology continues to evolve, we've also brought SAP Analytics Cloud to Apple Vision Pro. We also announced new data lake capabilities for SAP DataSphere. These new capabilities, including Object Store, Spark processing, and SQL on files, will be available starting in Q4 2024. Looking like I'll need to develop new CodeJam exercises and hit the road with them in 2025. I've done a lot of CodeJam focused on getting started with HANA ML capabilities, and I'm already excited that the development of intelligent data apps will become even more efficient with the introduction of the generative AI toolkit for SAP HANA Cloud. I hope you've seen this in the announcements.

Additionally, the new introduced Knowledge Graph engine in SAP HANA Cloud, combined with the accelerated vector engine, will be a powerful combo for building even more impactful AI-based applications using SAP data. So lots of work and fun ahead. Stay with us.

[Antonio] Hola, SAP developers. Many announcements in the integration space as part of SAP TechEd 2024. So we have new integration adapters to easily connect to ANAPLAN, CUPA, HubSpot, NetSuite ERP, Snowflake, and more. We also have a new message status overview monitor. That's the aggregated overview of the status messages, for example, that we had in SAP Process Orchestration that we all love. The migration tool has been enhanced. Now it's possible to do mass migration of integration scenarios. There's a new licensing option for SAP Integration Suite, which is called Starter Edition. This is focused for grow with SAP customers and SAP SuccessFactors customers. Also, we have new generative AI capabilities in SAP Integration Suite. So we can now optimize our groovy scripts in SAP Cloud Integration. And finally, the product, SAP Event Broker for SAP Cloud Applications, was recently renamed to SAP Cloud Application Event Hub. Apart from that, you can check out the documentation in CAP to see how you can easily connect your CAP application deployed to SAP BTP to the SAP Application Event Hub.

[Shrini] SAP announces several improvements to SAP Build. Its comprehensive solution for extending S/4 HANA Cloud and other line of business applications. The most significant updates enhance SAP Build by streamlining S/4 HANA extension developments, introducing new generative AI capabilities, and expanding access to about development tools. A new extension wizard available now lets developers access SAP Build directly from S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition. With this access, developers can extend custom fields, business logic, and processes, either on-stack or side-by-side. A critical feature of this integration is the knowledge of business context, such as events and objects, when creating business processes and while transitioning between S/4 HANA and SAP brand. This preserves important business information, enabling developers to switch between environments seamlessly without losing crucial context.

In Q4 2024, the extension wizard will let developers create SAP Fiori and SAPUI5 applications, leveraging the full set of features available in SAP Build. In Q4 2024, new capabilities such as code exploration and documentation search in SAP Build Code will reduce development time for Java and JavaScript developers. Low-code developers and business users will also be able to create user interfaces and automate workflows with assistance from Juul. Joule is SAP's generative AI assistant. In Q1 2025, ABAP developers will be able to generate high-quality code and unit tests with Joule that comply with ABAP cloud development model. Joule will also be able to generate explanations for legacy code, making it easier to modernize legacy code bases and migrate to a clean code. By end of Q4 2024, Joule will also be integrated into SAP Build Work Zone, a low-code tool for creating engaging and personalized business sites. Joule's generative AI capabilities will offer user task automation and intelligent support.

[Sheena] By end of 2024, developers will also be able to use low-code capabilities in SAP build to enhance Joule with new skills, integrating it seamlessly with both SAP and non-SAP applications. This flexibility ensures Joule can be tailored to meet customer-specific business needs. Finally, developers can create applications that feature alternative AI capabilities by taking full advantage of HANA Cloud to ground large language models using its vector engine. Soon, developers will also be able to achieve this S/4 HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine, scheduled for general availability in the first half of 2025. By the end of 2024, SAP Build will include access to ABAP development tools and ABAP development environments for SAP S/4 HANA Cloud and SAP BTP. New access from the SAP Build Lobby, the solution's central starting point for all extension projects, will make it easier for multi-disciplinary development teams, also known as Fusion Teams, to build extensions and custom processes across all technology stacks. Developers will also be able to create and monitor their web cloud projects directly from SAP Build. This integration streamlines workflows by reducing the need to switch between different environments, significantly improving development efficiency.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud - Deactivating users programmatically
entry.content:
Thanks to new features delivered with the Quarterly Release Update (QRC) in Q3 2024 the SCIM API now supports deactivating users.

This was a popular request and received a good number of votes in the Influence Portal (idea number 304030).

The ‘isActive’ user property was previously read-only, but now we can change this setting on a user basis via the user's endpoint for both version 1 and version 2 of the SCIM API.

 

Updated Sample Scripts

I have added 4 new sample scripts to my article ‘SAP Analytics Cloud User and Team Provisioning SCIM API Best Practices and Sample Scripts’ that enable you to automate the deactivation of users and teams of users.

The 4 new samples added are:

  • SCIM 1402-U-U-Ua-Fcj-Es-Update User Active status
  • SCIM 2402-U-U-Ua-Fcj-Es-Update User Active status
  • SCIM 1452-TU-U-Ua-Fcj-Es-Update Team Active status
  • SCIM 2452-TU-U-Ua-Fcj-Es-Update Team Active status

The samples 1xxx, use version 1 of the SCIM API, and the samples 2xxx, use version 2.

Samples x402 update a user's active status one at a time, while samples x452 update a whole team of users.

For those budding API enthusiasts, the version 2 samples use the PATCH and BULK operations.

 

Updated Scenarios

Whilst the samples above are helpful, the real value is my updated ‘scenarios’ that help manage users' life cycles, particularly the deactivation and eventual deletion of dormant users. A scenario is a straightforward concept comprising sets of pre-configured sample data files. Each scenario addresses a single use case by combining different sample scripts (Postman collections) in a particular order.

It means most of the thinking has been done for you. All you need to do is tweak the data files for your needs.

There are 5 updated scenarios for managing dormant users, as this topic is surprisingly complex. At a high level, this diagram shows what is possible.

 

The image above shows when users were created and their historic login activity. This table explains the logic determining if the user is dormant or not:

User 1

User-created over 3 months ago,without any logins since creation.The user is deactivated.

User 2

User-created under 3 months,without any logins since creation.No changes made

User 3

User-created over 3 months ago andhas 2 or fewer logins within the last 90 days.The user has no login within the last 30 days.The user is deactivated.

User 4

User-created over 3 months ago, buthas 3 logins within the last 90 days anda login within the last 30 days.No changes made

User 5

User-created over 3 months ago andhas 2 or fewer logins within the last 90 days, butthere was a login within the last 30 days.No changes made

User 6

User-created over 3 months ago, buthas 4 logins within the last 90 days, althoughno login within the last 30 days.No changes made

User 7

User-created over 6 months ago,without any logins since creation and is already deactivated.The user is deleted

User 8

User-created under 6 months,without any logins since creation and is already deactivated.No changes made

User 9

User-created over 6 months ago andhas 2 or fewer logins within the last 120 days.The user has no login within the last 60 days and is already deactivated.The user is deleted
User 10User-created over 6 months ago, buthas 3 logins within the last 120 days anda login within the last 60 days.The user is deactivated.
User 11User-created over 6 months ago andhas 2 or fewer logins within the last 120 days, butthere was a login within the last 60 days.The user is deactivated.
User 12User-created over 6 months ago,has 2 logins within the last 120 days, althoughno login within the last 60 days. The user is already deactivated.No changes made

 Please visit this updated article SAP Analytics Cloud – Managing dormant users for the next level of detail.

Alternatively, if you can't wait to get started, download the user guide which provides step-by-step instructions from this blog post (search for 'Sample Scripts User Guide').

entry.title: Perform the Iterative Simulation in SAC using Data Actions with blazing fast performance (Goal-Seek)
entry.content:
Need for Enhanced Simulation Capabilities in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)

In today's fast-paced and highly competitive business landscape, the ability to make informed, data-driven decisions is crucial for organizations aiming to differentiate themselves. One key aspect of effective decision-making is the capacity to evaluate the potential impacts of various choices before they are implemented. To this end, many managers are increasingly seeking tools that facilitate in-depth analysis of different outcomes based on varying assumptions.

Currently, while SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) provides robust analytics capabilities, it falls short in offering comprehensive iterative simulation functionalities. Features such as goal-seeking and what-if analysis are essential for businesses to assess the implications of their decisions dynamically. Goal-seeking allows users to determine the necessary inputs to achieve a desired outcome, while what-if analysis empowers them to explore various scenarios and their potential impacts on business performance

The absence of these simulation capabilities in SAC limits the ability of organizations to conduct thorough scenario planning and optimize their strategies effectively. As companies face complex challenges—ranging from market volatility to changing consumer preferences—the need for a solution that integrates simulation functionalities into SAC has become more pressing than ever.

By implementing enhanced simulation capabilities, SAC can enable managers to:

  1. Explore Multiple Scenarios: Evaluate how different variables impact outcomes, fostering a deeper understanding of potential risks and opportunities.
  2. Make Informed Decisions: Use data-driven insights to guide strategic choices, leading to more effective and optimized outcomes.
  3. Adapt to Changing Conditions: Quickly assess the ramifications of external changes, allowing businesses to pivot and respond proactively.

In conclusion, enhancing SAP Analytics Cloud with robust simulation functionalities such as goal-seek and what-if analysis is essential for empowering organizations to navigate today’s complex business environment. By equipping managers with the tools to simulate, analyse, and adapt their decisions, SAC can truly become an invaluable asset in optimizing business performance.

Although, we can achieve real-time simulations using Member Formulas, Story calculations and VDTs, we also need to understand, in xP&A world, we would need to have this capability to have the result of these simulations saved in our data model. So, when we use Data Actions for these functionalities, like simulations, Goal-seek or What if analysis, it would be very lengthy and time-consuming processes.

Refer:

Simulation on SAC with What-If analysis :  https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/what-if-analysis-with-sap-analytics-cloud-simulate-your-future-business/ba-p/13475295

Simulation on SAC Live Models : https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/what-if-simulation-in-a-sac-story-based-on-a-sac-live-model-e-g-sap-bw/ba-p/13525485

Simulation with VDT : https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/leveraging-sap-analytics-cloud-functionality-for-enterprise-planning/simulating-with-value-driver-trees_fc3552eb-ceb0-4f96-93e4-7c690bcaed2d

 

In any CAPEX decision, first thing they would like to see, is how is the current situation and how much they can benefit, if they improve on certain things. So, in nutshell, there will be certain conditions or constraints (inputs) and there will be some outcomes based on these inputs.

And businesses has to decide, what input they can afford, to get the desired outcome or vice versa.

Many businesses, use the Excel’s native function of Goal-Seek, which changes the Conditions (input) until the goal is achieved. and they start comparing the SAC solution with Excel and expect the simulation results in few seconds and also saved for use in other processes.

Goal-Seek is nothing but, If you know the result that you want from a formula but are not sure what input value the formula needs to get that result, use the Goal Seek feature.

This is known as iterative simulation. Where you simulate the results again and again until you reach to your goal by changing the input gradually in every iteration of simulation.

So Let us start how can we achieve the Iterative Simulation in SAC where we store the results of Every Iteration, and then find the goal. This Blog will focus mostly on Design, which can be implemented for any use case or scenario, where goal-seek is required.

In below example, for a power plant,

Goal is = Set NPV (Net Present Value) to 0

By Changing = Capacity Charge (Generation Cost - Input) X

It involves around 50+ Business logic to calculate the NPV from the Capacity Charge (generation cost).

Here Capacity Charge is critical business parameter, which decides the viability of project before we invest in project.

So below diagram explains how typically any Goal-Seek would work.

 

So now we understand that we can split the entire process in three areas,

  1. before goalseek (which are necessary to goalseek but may not be part of direct calculations of Goalseek KPIs.)
  2. GoalSeek KPIs itself
  3. After Goalseek

This way we can have better control over the Goalseek Process.

It is always necessary to ensure that we build data actions as per best practices, like scoping in every step, not leaving any dimension undefine, avoiding loops, choosing valid hierarchy etc so that we get smaller runtime and better performances.

Now let us assume that one of your goalseek Data Action is taking around the 80 seconds, so for one hundred iterations it would take around 8000 seconds. Which is almost a two hour and half.

This longer wait time makes business leaders disappointed and not user-friendly tool. However, we know that SAC analytics cloud runs on the SAP HANA database, here comes the power of SAP HANA Columnar processing capabilities. I have designed the solution which does the parallel simulation of these iterations and thus leveraging the SAP HANA processing with Innovative design of parallel processing of these iterations.

So, what we need to do is, we need to enable our system to perform the parallel processing of the all the iterations of Goalseek. Because the logic of Goalseek for all the iterations remain same.

Below Diagram explains how to parallelize the goalseek.

  1. First generate the required KPIs for Goalseek Calculations (which are not dependent of the user input Capacity Charge.)
  2. Perform Goalseek Calculations parallelly for multiple iterations (configurable)
  3. Find the correct Goal from above step 2 (Goalseek)
  4. Perform the next dependent steps (Optional)

 

 

 

Let us see steps. Technically now:

  1. Add the Iteration Dimension in your existing Model. (Where you want to parallelize the simulation results)

Since Goal-Seek Needs to perform N no. of iterations, we need to identify the iteration with the different identifier.

Example of Iteration Dimension:

 

In above example we have added the Iteration Identifier in Member IDs. But most importantly, we need to add the criteria of every iteration. Which is you want to change the certain input by x amount. In our case we have added the incremental change by 0.0000001 for every iteration member. See above table. You may add multiple properties similarly with different criterial of incremental change and you can achieve the different simulation possible with same data.

 

  1. Prepare the DATA

Default Value for all the data records in the Model after adding the ITERATION dimension will be #. Perform the Before Goalseek Preparatory steps (if any) and store all the results.

  1. Run Parallel Goalseek

Now we need to perform the Goalseek in parallel process.

So, we need to have the Pre-Requisite records generated at above step B, need to be replicated for no. of Iterations.

So, we need to create a Copy Steps from # iteration to multiple Iterations as shown in below screen.

 

  1. Execute the same Goalseek KPI Data Action step (which earlier was used to run for 80 seconds for single iteration) with required no. of iterations.

Now we need to mention the Iteration filter in

MEMBERSET [d/ZD_ITERATION] = BASEMEMBER([d/ZD_ITERATION], %Define_Goalseek_Iterations%)

 

 

 

Remember that we don’t want to run the GoalSeek KPIs for # Iteration. So, we are not including that in this step.

Here we have created a parameter for user to select the no. of iterations. User can select either 50 or 100 …. Iterations. And based on the member selected, DATA action will run for those many Iterations (Set of Data) parallelly. And will calculate the Goalseek KPI, which is NPV in our case.

One User Enters the Reference Capacity Charge (input) and we need to perform the Simulation (Goalseek KPIs) for multiple iterations, with different capacity charge, based on the user inputted initial value.

There fore When First time this user inputted value is used inside our data action logic, we need to change it for every iteration, based on its respective iterative change value we maintained in master data.

In our case we are changing it by 0.0000001, 0.0000002, 0.0000003….

Refer Iteration Dimension Screenshot and below Data Action screenshot.

 

And thus, we have run the Goalseek Simulation for all the possible combinations (no. or iterations in our case is 100) and the final value for all those 100 sets is calculated and stored against the respective iteration number.

  1. Identify the Correct NPV=0 (Goal)

Now we need to identify the correct Iteration Set where the required goal is achieved and same we need to copy to # Iteration so that we have the correct Goal in its respective capacity charge and Goalseek KPIs available for all other reporting and further calculations.

So, we need to Loop on each iteration to find the KPI NPV is value. Wherever we find the NPV=0, we will break the loop and capture the respective loop no. and store in Last Iteration Measure and Store the Correct Capacity Charge, which is User input capacity Charge less the respective Iterations’ reduction value from master data.

And lastly, we copy all results of that iteration set to # iteration, so that all further KPIs and reporting can be done.

Now the Data on non # Iteration can be deleted if business no longer wants it.

 

So Thus we can see that Even with Data Action Running for 100 Iterations, it will not take much time.

in our case, single iteration took around 70-80 sec, but with this parallel approach it takes only 2.5 min for 100 iterations, which means only 1.5 sec per iteration. in our case single iteration generates around 1.2 k records in 80 seconds, so 100 iteration it generated 122k records in just  2min. In total it improved performance by 98%

 

  1.
